---
title: "Student notebooks"
---

Run these documents in **RStudio** or **Positron** (or render with Quarto). The site uses **two parallel tracks**: synthetic **genes** (ground truth for teaching) and **Palmer Penguins** (one real dataset all week). See the **[Palmer Penguins data page](../dataset-penguins.qmd)** for context.

::: {.hub-muted}
Tip: use **Render** in the IDE, or from the project root run `quarto render notebooks/your-file.Rmd`.
:::

## Synthetic genes (known truth)

Ideal for understanding **LASSO, ridge, AIC**, and logistic regression when we **know** which simulated features matter.

- [linear-regression-lasso.Rmd](linear-regression-lasso.Rmd) — gene–trait simulation, visualization, `glmnet`, forward/backward AIC (`MASS::stepAIC`).
- [logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd](logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd) — simulated gene expression and disease status, `glm`, `rpart`, confusion matrix, decision boundaries.

## Palmer Penguins (continuity)

Same real table as the **Tuesday** `tidymodels` slide pipeline and the **Data** page.

- [penguins-body-mass.Rmd](penguins-body-mass.Rmd) — EDA, correlation / `ggpairs`, ridge and LASSO on **`body_mass_g`**.
- [penguins-classification.Rmd](penguins-classification.Rmd) — **Adelie vs Gentoo**: logistic regression, `rpart`, confusion matrix, 2D decision regions.
- [penguins-sex-classification.Rmd](penguins-sex-classification.Rmd) — predict **female vs male** from morphology ( **`species` omitted on purpose** ), logistic + `rpart`, stratified train/test.
- [penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd](penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd) — **three species**: `nnet::multinom`, multiclass `rpart`, confusion matrices + heatmap.

## Requirements

Install as needed: `palmerpenguins`, `tidyr`, `nnet`, `glmnet`, `caret`, `dplyr`, `GGally`, `ggplot2`, `knitr`, `MASS`, `rpart`, `rpart.plot`, `rcartocolor` (gene linear notebook theme).
